text:
1. **Remove the line.** Cleanest. MCP tools will then prompt for permission per-use (the default). Since the rule is already being skipped, this changes nothing about current behavior — it just clears the warning. [...] Assistant: Done. Removed the invalid `"mcp__*"` rule from `permissions.allow`.

That was the only issue `/doctor` reported. The fix clears the warning with no behavior change — the rule was already being skipped, so MCP tools were prompting per-use before and will continue to. If you later want to auto-allow specific MCP servers, the supported form is per-server: `mcp__<server>__*`.

The edit is valid JSON (I removed the trailing comma cleanly). Re-running `/doctor` should now come back clean.
